summary: Large Great Bay stage decomp pass centered on `src/melee/gr/grgreatbay.c`: replaced several `/// #` stubs with C, reconstructed a typed Great Bay stage-attribute table, expanded Great Bay ground-variable layouts, typed a few item/stage prototypes, and centralized a duplicated stage random-range helper. decomp.dev reported +2816 matched code bytes and 4 new full matches, while `.sdata` regressed slightly.

key_files.role: Main Great Bay implementation; added `grGb_StageAttr`, implemented or improved multiple `grGreatBay_801F....` functions, item spawning, callbacks, animation/collision logic, and random selection.
Expanded Great Bay ground variable structs with bitfields, child GObj array, Great Bay 4/Tincle state, and extra fields needed by new C implementations.
Added shared `ZRANDI` and `rand_range` helper using `HSD_Randi`, replacing duplicate per-stage inlines.
Typed Tincle helpers used from Great Bay: `it_802EBFAC(Item_GObj*)` and `it_802ECA70(HSD_GObj*) -> Item_GObj*`.
Removed local `rand_inline`; switched Izumi random timers to shared `rand_range`.
Removed local `rand_inline`; switched Kraid stage timing to shared `rand_range`.
Removed local `rand_inline`; switched Old Kongo timers to shared `rand_range`.
Removed local `rand_inline`; switched Old Yoshi guest timers to shared `rand_range`.

what_changed: Added a local `grGb_StageAttr` struct for Great Bay stage attributes, replacing `grGb_804D69E0.x0` from raw `s16*` with `grGb_StageAttr*`; fields remain offset-style names such as `x44`, `x68`, `x70`, and `x7C` because semantics are still mostly unknown.

Implemented `grGreatBay_801F4694`, which initializes a Great Bay ground object: sets map/animation, scales and translates its JObj from stage attributes, seeds timers via `rand_range`, initializes Great Bay flags/state, and enables ground flags.

Implemented a large state-machine body for `grGreatBay_801F499C`; it drives Great Bay object states `0..3`, animation transitions, hide/unhide flags, random timers, `mpLib` joint enable/disable calls, generator effects via `grLib_801C96F8`/`grLib_801C9874`, sound calls, rotation updates, and a call into `grGreatBay_801F63F4` for item spawning.

Implemented `grGreatBay_801F5460` and `grGreatBay_801F5600`, creating and managing a Tincle/Tingle-related item object through `it_802ECA70`, `it_802ECCA4`, `it_802ECC98`, `it_802EC830`, `it_802EBFAC`, and `it_802EBD14`, with stage collision joint updates and effects/sfx for item states.

Adjusted `grGreatBay_801F5914` to write collision callback accumulation into Great Bay fields `xD4`, `xD8`, and `xE0` instead of the old lower offsets, matching the expanded `grGreatBay_GroundVars` layout.
Refactored Great Bay child ground objects in `grGreatBay_801F5A00` from separate `gobj5`/`gobj6`/`gobj7`/`gobj8` fields into `gobjs[4]`, and reused `rand_range` against `grGb_804D69E0.x0->x2/x0`.

Implemented `grGreatBay_801F5AF8`, a timer/animation loop that starts animation on the main object and four child GObjs, tracks bitfield flags `x12.b0..b3`, resets animation when `lb_8000B134` indicates completion, and reseeds the timer.

Implemented `grGreatBay_801F5E28`, smoothing Great Bay object rotation and X/Y translation toward accumulated collision targets using stage-attribute factors, then applying `HSD_JObjSetRotationZ`, `HSD_JObjSetTranslateX`, and `HSD_JObjSetTranslateY`.

Implemented `grGreatBay_801F60C4`, a collision callback that scales incoming damage, computes impact distance from a JObj position, clamps rotation/displacement/Y offset using stage attributes, and accumulates response into `greatbay3` fields.
Reworked `grGreatBay_801F62F8` to use `grGb_StageAttr->x70[]` weighted random selection and `ZRANDI(total)` instead of raw `s16` indexes and manual zero checks.

Implemented `grGreatBay_801F63F4`, which checks a JObj spawn position against `Stage_GetBlastZoneBottomOffset`, uses stage attribute probability `x78`, filters weighted item entries in `x7C[10]` with `it_8026D324`, and spawns a selected item via `Item_80268B18`.
Added shared `ZRANDI(n)` and `rand_range(int a, int b)` to `gr/inlines.h` and removed duplicate `rand_inline` helpers from Izumi, Kraid, Old Kongo, and Old Yoshi stage files.
Typed item/stage headers so Great Bay can call Tincle and Great Bay helpers without `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`.

decomp.dev reported 4 new full matches: `grGreatBay_801F5600` (+788), `grGreatBay_801F4694` (+768), `grGreatBay_801F5E28` (+668), and `grGreatBay_801F5AF8` (+592). It also reported improvements for `grGreatBay_801F499C` to 99.90%, `grGreatBay_801F60C4` to 94.51%, `grGreatBay_801F63F4` to 96.63%, and `grGreatBay_801F5460` to 97.30%.
The bot also reported one regression: `main/melee/gr/grgreatbay` `.sdata` dropped from 100.00% to 81.82% (-5 bytes), so the PR improved code matching while leaving data matching to revisit.
smart_moves: Centralizing the duplicated `rand_inline` logic into `gr/inlines.h` avoided copy-pasted helpers across stage files and gave Great Bay a consistent `rand_range` implementation with a `ZRANDI` zero guard.

Reconstructing `grGb_StageAttr` made the Great Bay attribute table usable for timers, scales, movement factors, item weights, and probabilities without relying on brittle raw `s16` indexes like `data[56]`.
Keeping field names offset-based (`x68`, `x70`, `x7C`) was appropriate because the diff gives layout evidence but not enough semantic evidence for descriptive names.
Changing `greatbay2` child object storage to `gobjs[4]` allowed loops in `grGreatBay_801F5AF8` and reduced repeated code for GObjs 5 through 8.
Typing `it_802ECA70` as returning `Item_GObj*` and `it_802EBFAC` as taking `Item_GObj*` enabled direct, evidence-backed Great Bay/Tincle integration instead of propagating unknown signatures.
Using bitfields for `xC5` and `x12` captured the observed flag accesses (`b0123456`, `b7`, `b0..b3`) and likely contributed to matching control-flow/register behavior.

The code preserved exact magic constants for effects and sounds (`0x7531`, `0x7533`, `0x7534`, `0x7537`, `0x57E41`, `0x57E42`, `0x57E47`, `0x57E48`, `0x57E4A`) rather than inventing names without evidence.
decomp_lessons: When a stage data table is first understood structurally but not semantically, introduce a local offset-annotated struct and leave field names as `xNN` until asset or behavior evidence justifies better names.
Shared helpers should preserve exact edge behavior. `rand_range(a,b)` supports reversed min/max arguments and uses `ZRANDI` so zero ranges return 0 instead of calling `HSD_Randi(0)`.

Weighted random code often has assembly-significant ordering. `grGreatBay_801F62F8` totals `x70[0] + x70[1] + x70[3] + x70[2]` but subtracts in the order `x70[2]`, `x70[1]`, `x70[0]`, `x70[3]`; do not reorder for readability unless matching is verified.
Stage object state often spans `Ground` vars, child JOBjs, `mpLib` joints, animation, effects, and item APIs; matching requires typing all participating systems, not just the local stage file.
Small header type fixes can unlock large decomp progress when a stage calls item functions; avoid leaving `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` once callsites clearly establish the signature.
Bitfield layouts are useful matching tools for ground vars when code reads packed flags with specific bit ranges, as seen with `greatbay.xC5.b0123456/b7` and `greatbay2.x12.b0..b3`.
decomp.dev match reports can show mixed outcomes: this PR gained major code matches but broke `.sdata`; future work should inspect data-symbol ordering or constants after similar C conversions.
naming_conventions: Great Bay stage symbols use the `grGreatBay_801F....` address-name convention until functions receive semantic names.
The new stage-attribute type is named `grGb_StageAttr`, matching the existing Great Bay prefix `grGb_` and using offset fields (`x0`, `x2`, `x4`, etc.).
The global wrapper remains `grGb_804D69E0_t` / `grGb_804D69E0`, with only its pointer type improved from `s16*` to `grGb_StageAttr*`.
Ground variable structs continue the numbered convention: `grGreatBay_GroundVars`, `grGreatBay_GroundVars2`, `grGreatBay_GroundVars3`, and new `grGreatBay_GroundVars4`.
Homogeneous child GObjs were renamed from individually numbered members to `gobjs[4]`.
Unsemantic bitfields are named by bit positions/ranges, e.g. `b0123456`, `b7`, and `b4567`.
The random helper is named generically as `rand_range` in `gr/inlines.h`, replacing local stage-specific `rand_inline` helpers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(8)` in `grGreatBay_801F5AF8`, `grGreatBay_801F5E28`, and `grGreatBay_801F63F4`, and `PAD_STACK(12)` in `grGreatBay_801F60C4`, indicating stack-shape padding was needed for matching or near-matching.
Used a `ZRANDI(n)` macro to express `n != 0 ? HSD_Randi(n) : 0` compactly while preserving zero-range behavior and branch structure.
`grGreatBay_801F62F8` includes `current += 0;`, which appears to be a deliberate no-op matching artifact; keep such artifacts evidence-labeled and revisit only with asm diff proof.
Kept line-numbered `HSD_ASSERT` calls such as `HSD_ASSERT(529, jobj)`, `HSD_ASSERT(530, jobj2)`, `HSD_ASSERT(652, 0)`, `HSD_ASSERT(692, 0)`, and `HSD_ASSERT(1277, 0)`.
Used nested local blocks around temporaries such as `max`, `min`, and child `HSD_JObj* j`, likely to control lifetimes/register allocation for matching.
Preserved exact float constants including `1.5707964f`, `-1.5707964f`, `0.017453292f`, `15.0f`, `1000.0f`, `-50.0f`, and `10.0f` instead of replacing with named math constants.

In `grGreatBay_801F60C4`, the impact calculation uses `ABS(dx) / 15.0f`, clamps `t` to `1.0f`, then clamps accumulated rotation and displacement against attribute limits; this shape should be reused when matching similar collision-response callbacks.
Using arrays and loops (`gobjs[4]`, weighted `x7C[10]`) can improve both readability and matchability when the original code iterates over homogeneous stage data.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice, so reviewer intent/style feedback is not evidenced.
decomp-dev[bot] reported matched code at 62.01% (+0.07%, +2816 bytes) for GALE01 after the PR.
Bot reported a broken match in `main/melee/gr/grgreatbay` `.sdata`: 100.00% to 81.82% (-5 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Investigate remaining mismatch in `grGreatBay_801F499C` after 99.90% improvement; compare state-machine local scopes, assert lines, and `mpLib` call ordering.
Improve `grGreatBay_801F60C4` from 94.51%; focus on float temp ordering, `ABS(dx)`, casts around damage scaling, and stack padding.
Improve `grGreatBay_801F63F4` from 96.63%; inspect weighted item loop indexing (`offset / 4` vs `i`) and spawn struct copy order.
Resolve `main/melee/gr/grgreatbay` `.sdata` regression from 100.00% to 81.82%; inspect new `extern Vec3 grGb_803B81C8` and `extern SpawnItem grGb_803B81D4` placement/constants.
Name or document `grGb_StageAttr` fields once asset/stage-data semantics are known, especially `x70` path weights, `x78` spawn probability, and `x7C[10]` item kind/weight table.
Search for remaining duplicated per-stage random helpers that can safely move to `gr/inlines.h` using the same `rand_range`/`ZRANDI` semantics.
Audit Great Bay/Tincle item prototypes in `ittincle.h` against other callsites to ensure the new signatures are globally correct.
